name: CYNTHIA'S OF BEND
address: 1245 SE 3RD ST STE C-1, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Sidewalk Network; Utility Area; Roof System; Dumpster Enclosure
fr.considerations: Multiple Access Points; Limited Rear Access; Fenced Sections
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness at the property could benefit from maintaining clear access routes and ensuring unobstructed visibility of entrances to the main building. Modifying or clearly marking multiple access points would support a more efficient entry and exit strategy for responders. Reviewing the fenced sections to incorporate gates or openings may boost rear access, facilitating quicker movement around the property. Keeping pathways and the sidewalk network well-defined and readily accessible can aid in smooth navigation and optimal staging of personnel and emergency vehicles. Regularly inspecting and managing the landscaped areas to prevent potential obstructions along these routes could further enhance operational efficiency. Additionally, ensuring that the vehicle parking area remains orderly and free of impediments can positively impact responder movement and positioning.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Increasing clearance between vegetation and the structure may enhance defensible space and decrease fuel continuity. Trimming or strategically managing vegetation adjacent to the structure can help in reducing potential fire exposure. Promoting separation between scattered trees and the building, as well as within the vegetated perimeter, could facilitate a more effective defensible space. Encouraging the preservation of open terrain by maintaining a clear buffer around the property may aid in controlling fire spread. Implementing a routine management plan for existing vegetation could help maintain long-term wildfire resilience while improving overall property preparedness.
